title: Fix SQL Injection Error When Adding Salesforce Objects with Apostrophes
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to add Salesforce objects with special characters in field names without encountering errors so that all objects can be imported successfully.
description:
Adding Salesforce objects with apostrophes in field names (e.g., "Contact's Name") caused a fatal PDOException due to unescaped single quotes in the SQL query. The error occurred in `object.php` line 192.

### Root Cause
SQL query was using string concatenation instead of prepared statements, causing syntax errors when field values contained single quotes.

### Fix
- Fixed the SQL injection vulnerability by using parameterized queries
- Added try-catch error handling around all SQL queries to prevent raw database errors from being displayed to end users
- Errors are now logged server-side and a user-friendly message is shown instead
acceptance_criteria: Objects with apostrophes in field names can be added without errors
SQL queries use parameterized statements to prevent injection
Database errors are never displayed to end users
Errors are logged to the server error log
ux_changes: User-friendly error messages displayed instead of raw database exceptions.
middleware_changes: SQL queries converted to use prepared statements. Try-catch error handling added around database operations.
implementation_notes: The bug was in object.php line 192 where a raw SQL query used string concatenation. Fixed with PDO prepared statements. Also added global try-catch pattern for all SQL operations to prevent error information leakage.
comment_summary: Product owner requested adding try-catch error handling to all SQL queries to prevent exposing database internals. Developer identified and fixed the apostrophe issue, added error handling, and also fixed a related display issue. Contact object rendering was confirmed working after the fix.
